# Outpost CMS — Developer Reference

> A developer-first headless CMS built on PHP + SQLite with a Liquid-style template engine, a Svelte 5 admin SPA, and zero Composer dependencies. The admin panel, API, database, uploads, and cache all live inside a single `/outpost/` directory.

## Documentation

- [Full Developer Docs](/outpost/docs/): Complete reference for themes, template syntax, collections, members, API, and deploy
- [Template Syntax Reference](/outpost/docs/template-syntax/): All Liquid template tags
- [Collection Loops](/outpost/docs/loops/collection-loops.html): Looping over collection items with options
- [Flexible Content Loops](/outpost/docs/loops/flexible-content-loops.html): Layout builder loops with named layouts
- [Relationship Loops](/outpost/docs/loops/relationship-loops.html): Linked content from other collections
- [Conditional Logic](/outpost/docs/features/conditional-logic.html): Show/hide fields based on other field values
- [Channels](/outpost/docs/features/channels.html): External data sources (REST API, RSS, CSV) with template loops
- [Channel Loops](/outpost/docs/loops/channel-loops.html): Template syntax for channel data loops (type-agnostic)
- [Members System](/outpost/docs/members/): Member registration, login, password reset
- [REST API Reference](/outpost/docs/api/): All admin and member API endpoints
- [Deploy Guide](/outpost/docs/deploy.html): Apache, Nginx, file permissions, cache
- [Brand Settings](/outpost/docs/features/brand.html): Site-wide colors, fonts, logo, and favicon — baseline identity for themes
- [Theme Customizer](/outpost/docs/features/theme-customizer.html): Visual color, font, logo, and favicon editor with live preview
- [Auto-Updater](/outpost/docs/features/auto-updater.html): Built-in update system with theme updates
- [Theme Updates](/outpost/docs/themes/theme-updates.html): Managed theme auto-updates and conflict detection
- [Security](/outpost/docs/reference/security.html): Security hardening, rate limiting, SSRF prevention
- [Setup Wizard](/outpost/docs/features/setup-wizard.html): First-run setup wizard, content packs, getting started checklist, contextual tips
- [Forge](/outpost/docs/features/forge.html): Visual tag builder — select HTML, right-click or Cmd+E, wrap in Liquid tags with smart detection
- [Content Packs](/outpost/docs/themes/content-packs.html): JSON content packs for seeding starter content
- [Changelog](/outpost/docs/changelog.html): Every release and its changes
- [Roadmap](/outpost/docs/roadmap.html): What's shipped, what's next, and why

---

# Outpost CMS — Full Reference (llms-full)

## Stack

- PHP 8.x + SQLite (PDO) — no Composer, no frameworks
- Svelte 5 SPA (Vite 6) for the admin UI — built to `php/admin/`
- Liquid-style template engine that compiles `.html` templates to PHP and caches them
- TipTap for rich text editing, SortableJS for drag-and-drop reorder

## Admin URL

The admin panel is at `/outpost/`. The `index.php` at the outpost root serves the Svelte SPA by loading `admin/index.html` and rewriting asset paths. There is no `/outpost/admin/` URL for end users — everything goes through `/outpost/`.

## File Structure

```
your-site/
  outpost/                     # entire CMS
    admin/                     # built Svelte SPA (do not edit directly)
    themes/
      my-theme/
        index.html             # / route
        about.html             # /about
        blog.html              # /blog
        post.html              # /post/{slug} single item
        partials/
          head.html            # DOCTYPE through <body>
          nav.html             # site navigation
          footer.html          # </body></html>
        assets/
          style.css
        theme.json
    data/
      cms.db                   # SQLite database
    uploads/                   # media files
    cache/                     # compiled template cache + HTML page cache
    backups/                   # auto-created before each sync push
    api.php                    # admin REST API
    member-api.php             # member REST API
    sync-api.php               # builder sync API
    front-router.php           # PHP dev server router
    engine.php                 # core CMS functions (cms_text, cms_global, etc.)
    channels.php               # channel engine: sync, fetch, cache, template functions
    template-engine.php        # Liquid compiler (OutpostTemplate::render)
    mailer.php                 # SMTP / mail() email
    members.php                # member session helpers (OutpostMember)
    config.php                 # constants: DB path, SMTP, upload limits, etc.
    docs/
      index.html               # developer documentation
      llms.txt                 # this file
    member-pages/
      login.php
      register.php
      forgot-password.php
      reset-password.php
      verify-email.php
      logout.php
      profile.php
  index.php                    # Apache/Nginx front controller
```

## Routing

Rules evaluated in order:
1. Static files (images, CSS, JS) — served directly, no PHP
2. `/outpost/` paths — served directly by outpost (admin, API, docs, etc.)
3. `/sitemap.xml` — auto-generated sitemap
4. Collection URL patterns (e.g. `/post/{slug}`) — router extracts slug, sets `$_GET['slug']`, renders the single template
5. Channel URL patterns (e.g. `/listing/{slug}`) — same as collections but for external data channels
6. Path maps to theme template file (e.g. `/about` → `about.html`)
7. No match → 404

URL pattern example: pattern `/post/{slug}` with URL `/post/my-article` → template `post.html`, `$_GET['slug'] = 'my-article'`

Draft blocking: items with `status='draft'` return 404. Preview: append `?preview=TOKEN` (from `items/preview-token` API) to bypass draft blocking with a secure per-item token. Visibility enforcement runs before rendering (see Protecting Pages).

### Writing links in templates (IMPORTANT)

Links in templates must use **route paths** (the URL visitors see), never template filenames:

| Correct | Wrong | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `<a href="/">` | `<a href="index.html">` | `index.html` is the template file, `/` is the URL |
| `<a href="/about">` | `<a href="about.html">` | `/about.html` is not a valid route — returns 404 |
| `<a href="/blog">` | `<a href="blog.html">` | The router maps `/blog` to `blog.html` internally |

In collection loops use `{{ post.url }}` — Outpost generates correct URLs automatically. In menu loops use `{{ item.url }}` — already contains the configured route path.

**When converting static HTML sites to Outpost themes, update every `<a href="page.html">` to `<a href="/page">`.**

### Defining custom routes

Outpost uses file-based routing — there is no route registration function, no config file, no middleware pipeline. Creating a template file IS defining a route:

| You create | The route becomes |
|---|---|
| `events.html` | `/events` |
| `pricing.html` | `/pricing` |
| `contact.html` | `/contact` |
| `docs.html` | `/docs` |

Dynamic routes use collections: create a collection with URL pattern `/event/{slug}` and a template `event.html`. The router matches `/event/my-event`, extracts the slug, and renders the template with `{% single event from collection.event %}`.

**What you cannot do:**
- Multi-segment static routes like `/docs/getting-started` — template files map 1:1 to single-segment paths. Use query params (`/docs?page=getting-started`) or collection URL patterns instead.
- Custom PHP route handlers — the router is not extensible. For custom server-side logic, create a standalone PHP file (e.g. `/custom-api.php`) outside the theme.
- Route parameters beyond `{slug}` — collections and channels support only `{slug}` in URL patterns.

### Asset paths must be absolute

Always use absolute paths starting with `/` for CSS, JS, images, and fonts:
- Correct: `/outpost/themes/my-theme/assets/style.css`
- Wrong: `assets/style.css` (breaks on `/about`, `/post/hello-world`, etc.)

Relative paths resolve differently per page URL. On `/about`, `assets/style.css` becomes `/about/assets/style.css` — a 404.

## theme.json

Every theme requires a `theme.json` at the root of the theme directory. It provides metadata for the admin browser and optionally controls auto-update behavior.

```json
{
  "$schema": "/outpost/docs/schemas/theme.schema.json",
  "name": "My Theme",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "author": "Your Name",
  "description": "A clean theme for personal blogs.",
  "screenshot": "screenshot.png",
  "managed": true
}
```

| Field | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `$schema` | No | Path to JSON Schema for IDE autocomplete and validation. Use `"/outpost/docs/schemas/theme.schema.json"`. |
| `name` | Yes | Display name in admin theme browser |
| `version` | Yes | Semantic version badge on theme card |
| `author` | Yes | Creator name shown in details panel |
| `description` | Yes | Short summary shown in browser |
| `screenshot` | No | Relative path to PNG/JPG preview image |
| `managed` | No | When `true`, theme receives auto-updates during CMS updates. Reserved for bundled themes. |

### JSON Schema

Outpost ships a JSON Schema at `/outpost/docs/schemas/theme.schema.json` that enables IDE autocomplete and validation for `theme.json` files. Add `"$schema": "/outpost/docs/schemas/theme.schema.json"` to any theme.json for autocomplete of all fields including customizer sections, field types (`color`, `font`, `image`, `text`, `toggle`), and CSS variable names. All bundled themes include this reference.

The directory name is the slug (immutable); it is used as the `active_theme` setting value.

### Managed themes

Themes with `"managed": true` are official themes maintained by Outpost. They participate in the theme update system — when the CMS is updated, managed themes in the package are automatically updated with conflict detection. The bundled Personal and Starter themes ship with this flag.

Managed themes show a "Managed" badge in Admin → Themes. Duplicating a managed theme strips the `managed` flag — the copy becomes an independent user-owned theme that is never touched by updates. Do not add `"managed": true` to custom themes.

### Bundled themes

Outpost ships three managed themes:
- **Personal** — full-featured personal blog theme (reference implementation)
- **Starter** — clean, minimal starting point for non-developers
- **Skeleton** — developer reference theme with heavily commented templates demonstrating every template tag

The Skeleton theme is the recommended starting point for theme development. Duplicate it in Admin → Themes to create a new theme, then edit the copy. It demonstrates: output tags (text, richtext, image, link, textarea, color, number, date, select, toggle), globals, meta tags, wrapping and inline defaults, conditionals, collection loops with all options, single item fetch, menu loops with dropdowns, folder loops, pagination, form tag, includes, SEO block, and admin check.

### Brand Settings

Brand (Admin → Brand) sets site-wide visual identity: palette (primary, secondary, accent, neutral, background, surface), typography (heading font, body font, type scale ratio), and identity (logo, favicon). Stored in `content/data/brand.json`.

Brand values flow into themes via two paths:
1. **Customizer with `brand_key`** — customizer fields map to brand values using `"brand_key": "colors.accent"`. Priority: saved customizer value → brand value → theme.json default.
2. **Framework with `"framework": true`** — injects `--brand-*` CSS tokens and framework CSS directly. Theme CSS uses `var(--brand-primary)`, `var(--brand-font-heading)`, etc.

Available brand keys: `colors.primary`, `colors.secondary`, `colors.accent`, `colors.neutral`, `colors.background`, `colors.surface`, `typography.heading_font`, `typography.body_font`, `identity.logo`, `identity.favicon`.

#### Custom Google Fonts

The font picker in Brand and Theme Customizer ships with ~30 curated Google Fonts. Admins can add any Google Font via Brand → Typography → "Manage fonts". Enter the exact font name from fonts.google.com and choose a category (Sans Serif, Serif, Display, Monospace). Custom fonts are stored in the `settings` table as the `custom_fonts` key (JSON array of `{ name, category }` objects) and automatically appear in all font picker dropdowns.

API: `GET api.php?action=fonts` returns `{ fonts: [...] }` (custom fonts only). `PUT api.php?action=fonts` with body `{ fonts: [{ name, category }, ...] }` saves them. Requires `settings.*` capability.

### Customizer schema (optional)

Themes can declare customizable properties by adding a `customizer` key to `theme.json`. When present, the admin shows a visual Theme Customizer (Admin → Customize) that lets site owners change colors, fonts, logo, and favicon without editing code.

```json
{
  "name": "My Theme",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "customizer": {
    "sections": [
      {
        "id": "colors",
        "label": "Colors",
        "fields": [
          { "key": "color-accent", "label": "Accent Color", "type": "color", "default": "#14B8A6", "var": "--color-accent" }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "typography",
        "label": "Typography",
        "fields": [
          { "key": "font-heading", "label": "Heading Font", "type": "font", "default": "Inter", "var": "--font-display" }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "identity",
        "label": "Site Identity",
        "fields": [
          { "key": "logo", "label": "Site Logo", "type": "image" }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

Field types: `color` (hex picker, requires `var`), `font` (Google Fonts dropdown, requires `var`), `image` (media picker for logo/favicon). The `logo` key syncs to `@site_logo` global on save.

**Brand key mapping (optional):** Fields can include a `brand_key` property (e.g. `"brand_key": "colors.accent"`) to map to a Brand setting. When present, the brand value serves as the baseline default instead of the field's `default`. Value priority: Customizer saved value → Brand value → theme.json field default. The `GET customizer` API enriches fields with a `brand_default` property so the admin UI can show the brand value as the reset target. Fields without `brand_key` are unaffected (fully backward-compatible).

Storage: `content/data/customizer.json` — per-theme key, included in backups, safe from auto-updater. CSS overrides injected before `</head>` with `:root:not([data-theme="dark"])` selector (light mode only). Google Fonts loaded dynamically; hardcoded theme font links stripped.

Themes without a `customizer` key work normally — the admin shows "This theme doesn't support customization yet."

## Template Scanning & Auto-Rescan

`outpost_scan_theme_templates()` parses all `.html` template files in a theme and:
1. Creates page stubs in the `pages` table for each template
2. Creates field stubs in the `fields` table (never overwrites saved content)
3. Upserts into `page_field_registry` with field type, default value, and sort order

The scanner runs:
- On **theme activation** (`PUT themes/activate`)
- **Automatically on each frontend request** when any template file (top-level `.html` or `partials/*.html`) has been modified since the last scan

Auto-rescan uses `filemtime()` comparison against a `last_template_scan_<theme>` setting stored in the `settings` table. Editing templates via the code editor or direct file access triggers an automatic re-scan on the next frontend request — no theme re-activation needed.

The scanner extracts defaults from all syntax forms:
- Inline: `{{ field "Default" }}`, `{{ field | type "Default" }}`
- Closing-tag: `{{ field }}Default{{ /field }}`, `{{ field | raw }}Default HTML{{ /field }}`
- Global closing-tag: `{{ @field }}Default{{ /@field }}`, `{{ @field | type }}Default{{ /@field }}`

## Template Syntax — Complete Reference

### Output Tags `{{ }}`

All output tags register the field in the admin page editor automatically.

```html
{{ field_name }}              <!-- escaped text (default) -->
{{ field_name }}Default{{ /field_name }}    <!-- with wrapping default value -->
{{ field_name | raw }}        <!-- HTML as-is (rich text, TipTap output) -->
{{ field_name | raw }}<p>Default HTML</p>{{ /field_name }}  <!-- richtext with wrapping default -->
{{ field_name | image }}      <!-- image URL, opens media picker in admin -->
{{ field_name | link }}       <!-- URL value, opens link input -->
{{ field_name | textarea }}   <!-- multi-line text -->
{{ field_name | select }}     <!-- dropdown selector -->
{{ field_name | color }}      <!-- color picker -->
{{ field_name | number }}     <!-- numeric input -->
{{ field_name | date }}       <!-- date input -->
{{ field_name | toggle }}     <!-- boolean toggle -->
{{ field_name | or_body }}    <!-- text/excerpt fallback: uses auto-excerpt from body field if field is empty -->
{{ field_name | focal }}      <!-- focal point: outputs "X% Y%" for object-position/background-position -->
```

**Filter purpose:** Filters like `| image`, `| link`, `| textarea` do NOT transform the output. They tell the admin page editor which input type to render for that field (media picker, URL input, multiline textarea, etc.). The template output is always the stored value. `{{ hero | image }}` outputs the image URL string — wrap it in `<img src="...">` yourself.

Both inline (`{{ field "Default" }}`) and closing-tag (`{{ field }}Default{{ /field }}`) default syntax is fully supported. Closing-tag defaults also work with filters: `{{ field | raw }}<p>HTML</p>{{ /field }}`. Defaults are extracted by the template scanner and stored in `page_field_registry.default_value`, so the admin page editor pre-fills fields (including WYSIWYG richtext) with the template default when content is empty.

### Global Fields `{{ @field }}`

Site-wide settings shared across all pages. Appear in Admin → Globals.

```html
{{ @site_name }}
{{ @site_name | raw }}
{{ @logo | image }}
{{ @footer_text | textarea }}
```

### Meta Tags

```html
{{ meta.title }}Default Site Title{{ /meta.title }}
{{ meta.description }}Default description.{{ /meta.description }}
```

Outputs `<title>` and `<meta name="description">` tags.

On single item pages (`{% single ... %}`): `meta.title` automatically falls back to the item's `title` field if no page-level meta title is set. `meta.description` auto-generates a 30-word excerpt from the item's `body` field if no page-level meta description is set.

### Collection Loops

```html
{% for post in collection.post %}
  {{ post.title }}
  {{ post.slug }}
  {{ post.url }}             <!-- built from collection url_pattern -->
  {{ post.id }}              <!-- integer database ID -->
  {{ post.published_at }}
  {{ post.created_at }}
  {{ post.updated_at }}
  {{ post.body | raw }}      <!-- rich text -->
  {{ post.any_field }}       <!-- any custom field -->
{% else %}
  No posts found.
{% endfor %}
```

### Loop Options (space-separated after collection name)

| Option | Example | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `limit:N` | `limit:5` | Max N items |
| `orderby:field` | `orderby:published_at` | Order by field — always descending (DESC) |
| `paginate:N` | `paginate:10` | N items per page, enables `{% pagination %}` |
| `filteredby:param` | `filteredby:category` | Filter by `?param=slug` query string |
| `related:var` | `related:post` | Items from same collection excluding current single item |

Examples:
```html
{% for post in collection.post limit:3 orderby:published_at %}
{% for post in collection.post paginate:10 orderby:published_at filteredby:category %}
{% for item in collection.post limit:3 related:post %}
```

`paginate:N` and `limit:N` are mutually exclusive.

### Pagination

Place `{% pagination %}` after a loop that uses `paginate:N`. Renders `<nav>` with numbered links, Prev/Next, ellipsis. Preserves all query params (e.g. `?category=coding&page=2`).

```html
{% for post in collection.post paginate:10 %}
  ...
{% endfor %}

{% pagination %}
```

CSS classes: `.pagination`, `.page-link`, `.page-link.page-current`, `.page-ellipsis`

### Single Items

```html
{% single post from collection.post %}
  {{ post.title }}
  {{ post.body | raw }}
{% else %}
  Not found.
{% endsingle %}
```

Fetches by `?slug=` (set automatically by router). Related items inside a single:

```html
{% single post from collection.post %}
  {% for item in collection.post limit:3 related:post %}
    {{ item.title }}
  {% endfor %}
{% endsingle %}
```

### Channel Loops

```html
{% for item in channel.listings %}
  {{ item.title }}
  {{ item.description }}
  {{ item.price }}
{% else %}
  No listings available.
{% endfor %}
```

`listings` is the channel slug. Loop options: `limit:N` (max items), `orderby:field` (sort descending). Items come from cached external data. Syntax is type-agnostic — works the same for API, RSS, and CSV channels.

RSS feed example:
```html
{% for post in channel.blog_feed limit:10 %}
  <h2><a href="{{ post.link }}">{{ post.title }}</a></h2>
  <time>{{ post.pubDate }}</time>
  <p>{{ post.description }}</p>
{% endfor %}
```

CSV example:
```html
{% for member in channel.team %}
  <h3>{{ member.first_name }}</h3>
  <p>{{ member.role }}</p>
{% endfor %}
```

### Channel Single Items

```html
{% single listing from channel.listings %}
  {{ listing.title }}
  {{ listing.description }}
{% else %}
  Listing not found.
{% endsingle %}
```

Fetches by `?slug=` (set by channel URL pattern routing). Works identically to collection `{% single %}`.

### Navigation Loops

```html
{% for item in menu.main %}
  {{ item.label }}
  {{ item.url }}
  {{ item.target }}          <!-- _self or _blank -->
  {% for child in item.children %}
    {{ child.label }}
    {{ child.url }}
  {% endfor %}
{% endfor %}
```

`main` is the menu slug. Menu items support one level of nesting via `item.children`.

### Gallery Loops

```html
{% for img in gallery.photos %}
  {{ img.url }}
  {{ img.alt }}
  {{ img.caption }}
{% endfor %}
```

`photos` is the gallery field name on the current page or item.

### Repeater Loops

```html
{% for row in repeater.team_members %}
  {{ row.name }}
  {{ row.role }}
  {{ row.photo | image }}
{% endfor %}
```

`team_members` is the repeater field name. Each row has the sub-fields defined in the repeater schema.

### Flexible Content Loops

Flexible Content fields are ACF Pro-style layout builders. Define named layouts (e.g. Hero, CTA, Testimonial) each with their own sub-fields. Editors add, remove, and reorder layout blocks.

```html
{% for block in flexible.page_sections %}
  {% if block.layout == "hero" %}
    <section class="hero">
      <h1>{{ block.heading }}</h1>
      <p>{{ block.subheading }}</p>
    </section>
  {% endif %}
  {% if block.layout == "cta" %}
    <section class="cta">
      <h2>{{ block.title }}</h2>
      <a href="{{ block.button_url }}">{{ block.button_text }}</a>
    </section>
  {% endif %}
{% endfor %}
```

`page_sections` is the flexible content field name. Each block has a `block.layout` property identifying the layout type, plus all sub-fields defined for that layout. Sub-field values are auto-escaped (except richtext).

**Layout definition:** Layouts are defined in the collection schema builder as a JSON object. Each key is a layout name, each value is an object of sub-field definitions:

```json
{
  "hero": {
    "heading": { "type": "text", "label": "Heading" },
    "subheading": { "type": "textarea", "label": "Subheading" },
    "bg_image": { "type": "image", "label": "Background Image" }
  },
  "cta": {
    "title": { "type": "text", "label": "Title" },
    "button_text": { "type": "text", "label": "Button Text" },
    "button_url": { "type": "link", "label": "Button URL" }
  }
}
```

Supported sub-field types: text, textarea, richtext, image, link, select, color, number, date, toggle.

**Storage:** Flexible content is stored as a JSON array in the item's `data` column. Each entry has a `_layout` key (mapped to `block.layout` in templates) plus sub-field values.

**Engine function:** `cms_flexible_items(string $name): array` — decodes the JSON, escapes sub-field values (except `_layout` which becomes `layout`), returns an array of associative arrays.

### Relationship Loops

Relationship fields link content to items from another collection. Search and select items with reorder support. Stores item IDs, resolves full item data at render time.

```html
{% for item in relationship.related_posts %}
  <a href="{{ item.url }}">
    <h3>{{ item.title }}</h3>
    <p>{{ item.excerpt }}</p>
    <time>{{ item.published_at }}</time>
  </a>
{% endfor %}
```

`related_posts` is the relationship field name. Each item has all standard collection item fields: `id`, `slug`, `url`, `published_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, plus all custom fields defined in the related collection's schema.

**Schema options:**
- `collection` — slug of the collection to pull items from
- `multiple` — `true` (default) for multi-select, `false` for single item
- `max` — maximum number of items (0 = unlimited)

**Storage:** Stored as a JSON array of item IDs (integers) in the item's `data` column. Items are resolved at render time via a database JOIN — only published items are returned, in the order they were selected.

**Engine function:** `cms_relationship_items(string $name): array` — decodes the ID array, fetches full item records with collection URL patterns, escapes field values, returns ordered array of associative arrays.

### Folder Loops

```html
{% for label in folder.categories %}
  {{ label.name }}
  {{ label.slug }}
  {{ label.description }}
  {{ label.count }}           <!-- number of published items with this label -->
{% else %}
  <p>No categories yet.</p>
{% endfor %}
```

### `{% else %}` on All Loops

All loop types support `{% else %}` for empty-state fallbacks (v1.9.3). This includes: collection, channel, folder, menu, gallery, media folder, repeater, flexible content, and relationship loops. The `{% else %}` block renders when the loop has zero items.

### Conditionals

```html
{% if field_name %}...{% endif %}
{% if @global_name %}...{% endif %}
{% if item.field %}...{% endif %}
{% if item.field == "value" %}...{% endif %}
{% if item.field != "value" %}...{% endif %}
{% if field %}...{% else %}...{% endif %}
{% if admin %}...{% endif %}   <!-- true only when previewing in the admin editor -->
```

**Conditional limitations:** Outpost conditionals do not support `&&` (AND), `||` (OR), or `!` (NOT) operators. Each `{% if %}` tests a single expression. For compound logic, nest `{% if %}` blocks. There is no `{% elseif %}` — use nested `{% if %}` inside `{% else %}`.

### Includes

```html
{% include 'head' %}     <!-- loads partials/head.html -->
{% include 'nav' %}
{% include 'footer' %}
{% include 'sidebar' %}
{% include 'components/card' %}  <!-- loads partials/components/card.html -->
```

### Comments

```html
{# This is stripped at compile time — never appears in HTML #}
```

### Full Tag Reference

| Tag | Description |
|---|---|
| `{{ field }}` | Output field value (escaped) |
| `{{ field | raw }}` | Rich text / HTML unescaped |
| `{{ field | image }}` | Image picker |
| `{{ field | link }}` | Link input |
| `{{ field | textarea }}` | Multiline text |
| `{{ field | select }}` | Dropdown |
| `{{ field | color }}` | Color picker |
| `{{ field | number }}` | Number input |
| `{{ field | date }}` | Date input |
| `{{ field | toggle }}` | Boolean toggle |
| `{{ field | or_body }}` | Text with auto-excerpt fallback from body |
| `{{ field | focal }}` | Focal point as CSS `X% Y%` for `object-position` |
| `{{ @global }}` | Output global field |
| `{{ meta.title }}Default{{ /meta.title }}` | `<title>` tag (legacy — use `{% seo %}` in new themes) |
| `{{ meta.description }}Default{{ /meta.description }}` | `<meta name="description">` tag (legacy — use `{% seo %}` in new themes) |
| `{% seo %}` | Full SEO block: title, description, OG, Twitter Card, canonical, JSON-LD, robots |
| `{% for x in collection.slug %}` | Collection loop |
| `{% single x from collection.slug %}` | Single item by slug |
| `{% for x in folder.slug %}` | Folder label loop |
| `{% for x in menu.slug %}` | Navigation menu loop |
| `{% for x in gallery.field %}` | Gallery images loop |
| `{% for x in repeater.field %}` | Repeater rows loop |
| `{% for x in flexible.field %}` | Flexible content layout blocks loop |
| `{% for x in relationship.field %}` | Relationship items loop |
| `{% pagination %}` | Pagination nav |
| `{% if expr %}` | Conditional |
| `{% if admin %}` | True in admin editor preview |
| `{% else %}` | Else branch |
| `{% endif %}` / `{% endfor %}` / `{% endsingle %}` | Close blocks |
| `{% include 'name' %}` | Insert partial |
| `{# comment #}` | Template comment (stripped) |

## Partials

Stored in `partials/` inside the theme directory. Included via `{% include 'name' %}`. Subdirectories supported: `{% include 'components/card' %}` maps to `partials/components/card.html`. Each partial inherits the full template context from its parent.

Standard partial names: `head` (DOCTYPE through `<body>`), `nav` (navigation), `footer` (closing tags).

**Recommended `head.html`:** Use `{% seo %}` instead of manual `{{ meta.title }}` / `{{ meta.description }}` tags. The `{% seo %}` tag auto-generates `<title>`, meta description, Open Graph, Twitter Card, canonical URL, JSON-LD Article schema, and pagination links with a fallback chain:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  {% seo %}
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/outpost/themes/my-theme/assets/style.css">
</head>
<body>
```

## Theme Assets

Static files in `outpost/themes/my-theme/assets/` are served directly by the web server. Use absolute paths in templates:

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="/outpost/themes/my-theme/assets/style.css">
```

No asset pipeline or build step. Cache busting: add `?v=2` to URLs. No OUTPOST variable for theme path — hardcode the theme directory name or use a global field.

## Collections

A collection = a type of repeating content (posts, team members, projects, etc.).

Each collection has:
- `name` — display name (e.g. "Blog Posts")
- `slug` — used in templates (e.g. `post`) — immutable after creation
- `singular_name` — used in admin UI
- `url_pattern` — e.g. `/post/{slug}` — builds item URLs and routes single pages
- `schema` — field definitions

**Field types:** text, textarea, richtext, image, link, select, color, number, date, toggle, repeater, gallery, flexible, relationship.

**Setup workflow:** Unlike pages (where creating `about.html` instantly makes `/about` work), collections require two steps:
1. Create the collection in Admin → Collections (set name, slug, URL pattern, add schema fields)
2. Create the template files — a list page (e.g. `blog.html`) and a single page (e.g. `post.html`). The single template filename **must match the collection slug**: slug `post` → `post.html`, slug `portfolio` → `portfolio.html`.

If the template exists but the collection hasn't been created in admin, `{% for item in collection.post %}` renders nothing (no error). If the collection exists but the template doesn't, visiting an item URL returns 404.

Items have statuses: `published`, `draft`, `scheduled`, `pending_review`. Only `published` items with `published_at` in the past appear in loops and single templates. Items with `pending_review` status are treated as drafts (not visible to visitors).

Single template naming: URL pattern `/post/{slug}` → template file `post.html` (first path segment).

### Conditional Logic

Collection schema fields support conditional logic — show/hide fields based on other field values. Conditions are evaluated in real-time in the editor; hidden fields are not rendered.

**Operators:**
| Operator | Description |
|---|---|
| `==` | Equals (string compare; also matches `true`/`1` and `false`/`0`/empty) |
| `!=` | Not equal |
| `not_empty` | Has any value (not empty, not `0`, not `false`) |
| `empty` | Is empty, `0`, or `false` |

**Schema definition:** Conditions are stored per-field in the collection's `schema` JSON:

```json
{
  "category": { "type": "select", "label": "Category", "options": "news,event,product" },
  "event_date": {
    "type": "date",
    "label": "Event Date",
    "conditions": [
      { "field": "category", "operator": "==", "value": "event" }
    ]
  }
}
```

When `category` is not "event", the `event_date` field is hidden from the editor. Multiple conditions use AND logic (all must be true). Conditional logic is editor-side only — it does not affect template rendering or API output.

## Scheduling

Set a future publish date on any collection item. Outpost uses lazy publishing — no cron required.

When an item is saved with `status = 'scheduled'` and a `scheduled_at` timestamp, it is invisible to visitors. On every incoming page request, Outpost promotes any scheduled items whose `scheduled_at` is in the past to `published`.

To use: open item → set Status to "Scheduled" → pick date/time → Save.

**Status transitions:**

| From | To | How |
|---|---|---|
| draft | published | Manual in admin |
| draft | pending_review | Editor publishes on require_review collection |
| draft | scheduled | Set Scheduled + pick date |
| pending_review | published | Admin approves |
| pending_review | draft | Admin rejects |
| scheduled | published | Automatic on next page request, or manual |
| scheduled | draft | Manual cancel |
| published | draft | Manual unpublish |

**Time zones:** Scheduled times are stored as UTC. Set time zone in `config.php`:
```php
date_default_timezone_set('America/New_York');
```

**For low-traffic sites** add a cron job to trigger the lazy check:
```
*/5 * * * * curl -s https://yourdomain.com/ > /dev/null 2>&1
```

## Editorial Workflow

Collections can require review before publishing via the `require_review` toggle in the collection schema editor.

**How it works:**
- When `require_review` is enabled on a collection, editors who click "Publish" will have their item set to `pending_review` instead of `published`
- Admins and super_admins can always publish directly, even on require_review collections
- Items in `pending_review` are not visible to site visitors (treated same as drafts)
- Admins approve or reject pending items via the collection items list (bulk or individual)
- Approving sets the item to `published` with `reviewed_by` and `reviewed_at` timestamps
- Rejecting returns the item to `draft` status

**API endpoints:**
- `PUT items/approve` — approve items (admin+ only), body: `{ ids: [1,2,3] }`
- `PUT items/reject` — reject items (admin+ only), body: `{ ids: [1,2,3] }`
- `PUT items/bulk-schedule` — schedule items, body: `{ ids: [1,2,3], scheduled_at: "2026-03-15T09:00:00" }`
- `GET items/labels-with-counts?collection=slug` — returns all folders for a collection with labels and per-label item counts, plus `total_items` and `unfiled_count`
- `POST items/bulk-labels` — bulk add/remove label assignments, body: `{ item_ids: [1,2,3], label_id: 5, action: "add"|"remove" }`
- `GET items?collection=slug&label_id=N` — filter items by label ID; use `label_id=unfiled` for items with no label assignments
- `GET calendar?start=YYYY-MM-DD&end=YYYY-MM-DD&collection=slug` — items within date range for editorial calendar

**Webhook events:** `entry.submitted_for_review`, `entry.approved`, `entry.rejected`

**Database columns added:**
- `collections.require_review` (INTEGER DEFAULT 0)
- `collection_items.reviewed_by` (INTEGER, user ID)
- `collection_items.reviewed_at` (TEXT, ISO timestamp)

## Folders

> Renamed in v1.0.0-beta.7: "Taxonomies" → **Folders**, "Terms" → **Labels**. Old syntax (`{% for term in taxonomy.slug %}`) and old API endpoints (`action=taxonomies`, `action=terms`) still work for backward compatibility.

Create in Admin → Folders. Associate with a collection. Add labels. Types: `flat` (peer labels) or `hierarchical` (parent/child).

```html
{% for label in folder.categories %}
  <a href="/blog?category={{ label.slug }}">{{ label.name }}</a>
{% endfor %}

{% for post in collection.post filteredby:category %}
  ...
{% endfor %}
```

Access item's labels inside a loop or single: `item.labels.category` (array).

## Global Fields

Site-wide. Admin → Globals. Use `{{ @field_name }}` in templates. Auto-scaffolded from templates on first load. Conditional rendering: `{% if @field_name %}`.

Storage: key-value pairs in the `settings` table. Saving globals clears all HTML caches.

## Media

Upload files through Admin → Media or via image field pickers in page/item editors.

**Supported types:** jpg, jpeg, png, gif, webp, avif, svg, pdf, mp4, webm, mp3, wav

**Processing (raster images only):**
- Images wider than 2400px are scaled down to 2400px (aspect ratio preserved)
- JPEG and PNG auto-converted to WebP (configurable, enabled by default)
- Thumbnail generated at max 400×300px with `thumb_` prefix
- Pixel dimensions stored in database
- Filenames sanitized + Unix timestamp prefix added

**WebP auto-conversion:**
- Converts JPEG and PNG uploads to WebP using GD (25–50% smaller)
- Skips GIF (animation), SVG (vector), WebP (already optimized), AVIF, PDF, video
- Preserves PNG alpha/transparency
- Original file replaced on disk with `.webp` extension
- Disable: `define('OUTPOST_WEBP_AUTO_CONVERT', false);`
- Quality: `define('OUTPOST_WEBP_QUALITY', 85);` (0–100, default 85)
- Existing files are not retroactively converted

**Focal point:**
- Every image has a focal point (default: 50% 50% center)
- Set by clicking on the image preview in the media detail panel
- Stored as `focal_x` and `focal_y` (0–100) in the database
- Template filter: `{{ field | focal }}` outputs `X% Y%` for CSS
- Use with `object-position` or `background-position`:
```html
<img src="{{ hero_image | image }}" style="object-fit: cover; object-position: {{ hero_image | focal }};">
```
- Works on collection items: `{{ post.featured_image | focal }}`
- Returns `50% 50%` if no custom focal point set

**Media folders:**
- Organize files into folders (up to 3 levels of nesting)
- **Multi-folder assignment:** Files can belong to multiple folders simultaneously (junction table)
- Each folder has an auto-generated unique slug for use in template tags
- Folder sidebar in media library: All Files, Unfiled, folder tree
- Create, rename, delete folders via right-click context menu
- **Bulk folder creation:** Type comma-separated names to create up to 50 folders at once
- Drag files from grid onto folders to **add** (not move — files keep existing assignments)
- Uploads auto-assigned to the currently active folder
- Deleting a folder: files become unfiled, children re-parented
- **Resizable detail sidebar:** Drag left edge to resize (220–500px), width persisted in localStorage
- **Right-click context menu on files:** Shows folder badges, quick "Add to folder", Copy Path, Delete
- Folders are logical — files stay in `uploads/` on disk, URLs unchanged
- **Role-based folder restrictions:** Editors can be scoped to specific folders via per-user grants (Admin → Users → Edit → Media Folder Access). No grants = full access. Admins/super_admins always have full access. Restricted editors cannot create/rename/delete folders.
- API: `GET/POST/PUT/DELETE api.php?action=media-folders`
- Bulk create: `POST api.php?action=media-folders/bulk` with `{names: [...], parent_id: null}`
- Move/add/remove: `PUT api.php?action=media/move` with `{ids: [...], folder_id: N, action: "set"|"add"|"remove"}`
- Get folders for item: `GET api.php?action=media/folders&media_id=N`
- Assign folders: `PUT api.php?action=media/assign-folders` with `{media_id: N, folder_ids: [...]}`
- Folder grants: `GET/PUT api.php?action=users/media-folder-grants` with `{user_id: N, folder_ids: [...]}`
- Filter media list: `GET api.php?action=media&folder_id=N` (or `unfiled`)

**Gallery from media folders (template tag):**
```html
{% for img in media_folder.photos %}
  <img src="{{ img.url }}" alt="{{ img.alt }}"
       style="object-position: {{ img.focal_x }}% {{ img.focal_y }}%;">
{% endfor %}
```
- `photos` is the folder slug (auto-generated, unique, visible via right-click → Copy template tag)
- Available fields: `img.url`, `img.alt` (or `img.alt_text`), `img.width`, `img.height`, `img.focal_x`, `img.focal_y`, `img.mime_type`, `img.filename`
- Images returned in reverse-chronological order (newest first)
- All string fields are HTML-escaped

**Bulk upload:**
- Drop multiple files or select multiple in file picker
- Upload queue drawer (bottom-right) with per-file progress bars
- 2 concurrent uploads at a time
- Shows WebP savings per file
- Cancel individual uploads while in progress
- Auto-collapses 3s after all complete

**Storage path:** `outpost/content/uploads/` (flat, no subdirectories). A symlink at `outpost/uploads` points to `content/uploads/` for URL compatibility.

```
outpost/content/uploads/1700000000_hero-photo.webp        # original (converted)
outpost/content/uploads/thumb_1700000000_hero-photo.webp   # thumbnail
```

URL stored in DB (and output by `| image`): `/outpost/uploads/1700000000_hero-photo.webp` (served via symlink)

**SVG sanitization:** `<script>` tags, `on*` event handlers, and external `xlink:href` references are removed before saving.

**In templates:**
```html
<img src="{{ hero_image | image }}" alt="{{ hero_title }}">
<img src="{{ hero_image | image }}" style="object-fit: cover; object-position: {{ hero_image | focal }};">
{{ @logo | image }}
<a href="{{ brochure_pdf | link }}" download>Download</a>
```

**Max upload size:** 10 MB default. Override in `config.php`:
```php
define('OUTPOST_MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE', 25 * 1024 * 1024);
```

Never delete `outpost/content/uploads/` — not regenerated automatically.

## Members

Built-in member system for front-end user accounts. Separate from admin users.

**Member roles:** `free_member`, `paid_member`

**Member status:** `active` (default), `suspended` (blocked from login). Manage in Admin → Members.

Member pages at `outpost/member-pages/`:
- `login.php` — sign in (shows resend-verification button for unverified members)
- `register.php` — new account (shows "check your email" when verification enabled)
- `forgot-password.php` — request reset email
- `reset-password.php` — set new password via token
- `verify-email.php` — email verification landing page (validates token, marks email verified)
- `logout.php` — sign out
- `profile.php` — view/edit member profile

**Email verification** (optional): Enable in Admin → Settings → Members. When on, new members receive a verification email with a 24-hour token link. They cannot sign in until verified. Members registered before the setting was enabled are grandfathered in. Admins can manually verify from Admin → Members. Setting key: `require_email_verification` ('0'/'1').

## Protecting Pages

### Quick start — checking member authentication

```php
<?php
require_once $_SERVER['DOCUMENT_ROOT'] . '/outpost/members.php';

// Check if ANY member is logged in (returns true/false)
$isLoggedIn = OutpostMember::check();

// Get the full member record (returns array or null)
$member = OutpostMember::current();

if (!$member) {
    header('Location: /outpost/member-pages/login.php?redirect=' . urlencode($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']));
    exit;
}

// Check specific role
if ($member['role'] === 'paid_member') {
    // Paid member — show premium content
}

// Check if membership has expired
if ($member['member_expires'] && strtotime($member['member_expires']) < time()) {
    // Expired — prompt renewal
}

// Available member fields:
// $member['id'], $member['email'], $member['display_name'],
// $member['role'], $member['status'], $member['member_expires'],
// $member['created_at']
?>
```

The member system works out of the box. No configuration is needed beyond having Outpost installed. The `members.php` file handles sessions, cookie validation, and database lookups automatically. Just `require_once` it and call the static methods.

### Page-level visibility

Every page in Admin → Pages has a visibility setting:

| Setting | Who can access |
|---|---|
| `public` | Anyone (default) |
| `members` | Any logged-in member |
| `paid` | `paid_member` role only |

Enforced by `front-router.php` and `index.php` before template renders. Non-public pages redirect to login with `?redirect=` param.

### Template-level protection (PHP partial)

```php
<?php
// partials/member-gate.php
require_once $_SERVER['DOCUMENT_ROOT'] . '/outpost/members.php';
if (!OutpostMember::check()) {
    header('Location: /outpost/member-pages/login.php?redirect=' . urlencode($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']));
    exit;
}
?>
```

Include in Liquid template:
```html
{% include 'member-gate' %}
<!-- rest of template renders only for logged-in members -->
```

Paid-only gate:
```php
<?php
require_once $_SERVER['DOCUMENT_ROOT'] . '/outpost/members.php';
$member = OutpostMember::current();
if (!$member) {
    header('Location: /outpost/member-pages/login.php?redirect=' . urlencode($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']));
    exit;
}
if ($member['role'] !== 'paid_member') {
    header('Location: /upgrade');
    exit;
}
?>
```

### OutpostMember methods

```php
OutpostMember::check()           // bool — is a member logged in?
OutpostMember::current()         // array|null — full member record or null
// member fields: id, email, display_name, role, member_expires, status
```

## Admin Roles

Admin users have one of four roles (separate from front-end member roles):

| Role | Description |
|---|---|
| `super_admin` | Full access + user management + site settings + destructive ops. Reserved for site owner. |
| `admin` | Full content + themes + code editor + user management. Cannot change site-level settings. |
| `developer` | Full content + code editor + theme files. No user/member management or settings. |
| `editor` | Content only (pages, collections, media, globals, navigation, folders, form submissions). No themes, code, analytics, channels, form builder, settings, or user management. Can be restricted to specific collections. Cannot edit locked pages. |

### Collection-Scoped Editors

Editors can be restricted to specific collections by an admin:

1. Go to Admin → Users → [Editor User]
2. Under "Collection Access", check the collections the editor should access
3. If no boxes checked = unrestricted access (backward compatible)

When restricted: only granted collections appear in sidebar, API returns 403 for non-granted collection operations (list, create, update, delete items, bulk ops).

Grants stored in `user_collection_grants` table. API: `GET/PUT users/grants?user_id=N`.

### Per-Page Locks

Admins can lock individual pages to prevent editing by non-admin roles:

- `super_admin` and `admin` can lock/unlock pages and always edit locked pages
- `developer` and `editor` see a "locked" banner and cannot edit or delete locked pages (API returns 403)
- Lock set via "Lock page" checkbox in Page sidebar tab
- Locked pages show lock icon in pages list
- `locked` field only settable by admin+; silently stripped for other roles

## Password Reset

### Admin users

1. "Forgot password?" on login page → `POST /outpost/api.php?action=auth/forgot` with `{ email }`
2. API generates `bin2hex(random_bytes(32))` token, stores with 1-hour expiry, emails reset link
3. Reset URL: `/outpost/?reset_token=TOKEN`
4. Login SPA detects `?reset_token=` on mount, shows reset view, strips token from URL via `history.replaceState`
5. `POST /outpost/api.php?action=auth/reset` with `{ token, password }` → token NULLed, password updated

### Members

1. `forgot-password.php` → `POST /outpost/member-api.php?action=forgot`
2. Reset URL: `/outpost/member-pages/reset-password.php?token=TOKEN`
3. `reset-password.php` validates token on load, shows form, updates on POST

Security: tokens are 64-char hex, 1-hour expiry, single-use, endpoint always returns 200 (no email enumeration).

## Forms

### Visual Form Builder

Create forms visually in Admin → Forms. The builder provides 14 field types: text, email, phone, url, number, textarea, select, radio, checkbox, date, time, hidden, html, section.

Render a builder form in any template:

```html
{% form 'contact' %}
```

This outputs a complete `<form>` with proper field types, labels, validation, honeypot spam protection, and submit button. No JavaScript required. Outpost injects no default form styles — your theme CSS has full control.

**Rendered HTML structure** (example with name, email, message fields):

```html
<form method="POST" action="/outpost/form.php" class="outpost-form outpost-form--contact">
  <input type="hidden" name="_form" value="contact">
  <input type="hidden" name="_redirect" value="/contact?sent=1">
  <input type="hidden" name="_form_id" value="3">
  <!-- honeypot (hidden from real users) -->
  <div style="position:absolute;left:-9999px" aria-hidden="true">
    <input type="text" name="_hp_website" tabindex="-1" autocomplete="off">
  </div>
  <div class="outpost-field outpost-field--text">
    <label for="outpost-f-name">Name <span class="outpost-required">*</span></label>
    <input type="text" id="outpost-f-name" name="name" required placeholder="Your name">
  </div>
  <div class="outpost-field outpost-field--email">
    <label for="outpost-f-email">Email <span class="outpost-required">*</span></label>
    <input type="email" id="outpost-f-email" name="email" required>
  </div>
  <div class="outpost-field outpost-field--textarea">
    <label for="outpost-f-message">Message</label>
    <textarea id="outpost-f-message" name="message" rows="5"></textarea>
    <p class="outpost-field-desc">We typically respond within 24 hours.</p>
  </div>
  <button type="submit" class="outpost-form-submit">Send Message</button>
</form>
```

**Field settings:** label, machine name, required, placeholder, description, default value, CSS classes. Choice fields (select/radio/checkbox) support `Label|value` pairs. Number fields support min/max/step.

**Form settings:** submit button label, honeypot toggle, confirmation type (message or redirect URL).

**Submissions:** View in Admin → Submissions. Star, add notes, bulk actions (mark read, star, archive, delete), filter by form and status.

### Manual HTML Forms

You can also hand-code forms. Point form action at `/outpost/form.php`. Use hidden `_form` and `_redirect` fields:

```html
<form action="/outpost/form.php" method="POST">
  <input type="hidden" name="_form" value="contact">
  <input type="hidden" name="_redirect" value="/contact?sent=1">
  <input type="hidden" name="_notify" value="you@example.com"> <!-- optional override -->
  <input type="text" name="name" required>
  <input type="email" name="email" required>
  <textarea name="message"></textarea>
  <button type="submit">Send</button>
</form>
```

reCAPTCHA: add a hidden `g-recaptcha-response` field — `form.php` validates it automatically if a secret key is configured in settings.

**URL pre-population:** Form fields auto-fill from query parameters (e.g. `?name=John` pre-fills the name field).

### CSS Classes

Builder forms use `outpost-form`, `outpost-field`, `outpost-field--{type}`, `outpost-form-submit`. Style with:

```css
.outpost-form { /* form wrapper */ }
.outpost-field { /* field wrapper */ }
.outpost-field label { /* field label */ }
.outpost-field input, .outpost-field textarea, .outpost-field select { /* inputs */ }
.outpost-required { /* required asterisk */ }
.outpost-field-desc { /* help text */ }
.outpost-form-submit { /* submit button */ }
```

## Channels (External Data Sources)

Channels connect Outpost to external data sources — REST APIs, RSS feeds, and CSV files. If Collections are content you create in the admin, Channels are content that flows in from somewhere else. No plugins, no Composer, no build step — connect to any JSON API, RSS/Atom feed, or CSV file, cache data in SQLite, and render it in templates with the same Liquid syntax.

### Concept

- **Collections** = content you create and edit in the admin panel
- **Channels** = content that flows in from external sources (read-only in Outpost)
- Three source types: **REST API**, **RSS Feed** (RSS 2.0 + Atom 1.0), **CSV** (any URL serving CSV data)
- All three types use identical template syntax: `{% for item in channel.slug %}` — templates are type-agnostic
- Both collections and channels support URL patterns for single-item pages
- Channel data is cached in SQLite with configurable TTL
- Type is chosen at creation and cannot be changed afterward

### Creating a Channel

Navigate to Admin → Channels → New Channel. Choose the source type (REST API / RSS Feed / CSV), then the 4-step builder wizard:

1. **Connect** — choose source type, enter URL, configure type-specific options (see below), test connection
2. **Schema** — view discovered fields, set data path (API only), pick fields to include, set ID and slug fields
3. **Configure** — name, slug, cache TTL (5min/15min/1hr/6hr/daily/manual), max items, sort field, URL pattern
4. **Preview** — see cached items in table, get auto-generated template code

**REST API** connect options: URL, HTTP method, auth method (None/API Key/Bearer Token/Basic Auth), custom headers, query parameters.

**RSS Feed** connect options: Feed URL, optional auth. Auto-detects RSS 2.0 vs Atom 1.0. Fields auto-mapped (see RSS Field Mapping below).

**CSV** connect options: CSV URL, delimiter (comma/tab/semicolon/pipe), "first row is headers" toggle. Headers auto-sanitized to lowercase with underscores.

### Authentication Types

| Type | How it works |
|---|---|
| None | No authentication headers sent |
| API Key | Custom header name + value (e.g. `X-API-Key: abc123`) |
| Bearer Token | `Authorization: Bearer {token}` header |
| Basic Auth | `Authorization: Basic {base64(user:pass)}` header |

### Config JSON Structure

The `config` column stores all connection settings as JSON:

```json
{
  "url": "https://api.example.com/v1/listings",
  "method": "GET",
  "auth_type": "bearer",
  "auth_config": { "bearer_token": "sk_live_..." },
  "headers": { "Accept": "application/json" },
  "params": { "status": "active", "limit": "100" },
  "data_path": "data.listings",
  "id_field": "id",
  "slug_field": "slug",
  "pagination": {
    "type": "offset",
    "page_param": "page",
    "limit_param": "limit",
    "per_page": 50,
    "cursor_param": "after",
    "cursor_path": "meta.next_cursor"
  }
}
```

### RSS Field Mapping

RSS channels auto-map standard feed fields. Both RSS 2.0 and Atom 1.0 are supported:

| Field | RSS 2.0 source | Atom 1.0 source |
|---|---|---|
| `title` | `<title>` | `<title>` |
| `link` | `<link>` | `<link rel="alternate" href="...">` |
| `description` | `<description>` | `<summary>` |
| `pubDate` | `<pubDate>` | `<published>` or `<updated>` |
| `author` | `<author>` or `<dc:creator>` | `<author><name>` |
| `content` | `<content:encoded>` | `<content>` |
| `category` | `<category>` (comma-joined) | `<category term="...">` |
| `guid` | `<guid>` | `<id>` |
| `enclosure_url` | `<enclosure url="...">` | — |

Default ID field: `guid`. Default slug source: `title`.

### CSV Field Mapping

CSV channels use column headers as field names. Headers are sanitized: lowercase, spaces replaced with underscores, special characters removed. If "first row is headers" is disabled, columns are named `col_0`, `col_1`, etc.

Supported delimiters: comma (default), tab, semicolon, pipe. UTF-8 BOM is stripped automatically.

**Google Sheets tip:** Use File → Share → Publish to web → CSV. The URL looks like `https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/.../pub?output=csv`.

### Data Path Extraction (REST API only)

Dot-notation to target the array in the API response:
- `data.listings` → `response['data']['listings']`
- `results` → `response['results']`
- `data.items.records` → `response['data']['items']['records']`
- Empty string → response root (when API returns a plain array)

### Channel Loops

```html
{% for item in channel.listings %}
  <h2>{{ item.title }}</h2>
  <p>{{ item.description }}</p>
  <span>{{ item.price }}</span>
{% else %}
  <p>No listings available.</p>
{% endfor %}
```

Loop options (space-separated after channel name):

| Option | Example | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `limit:N` | `limit:5` | Show at most N items |
| `orderby:field` | `orderby:price` | Sort by field (descending) |

Combined: `{% for item in channel.listings limit:6 orderby:updated_at %}`

### Single Channel Items

```html
{% single listing from channel.listings %}
  <h1>{{ listing.title }}</h1>
  <p>{{ listing.description }}</p>
{% else %}
  <p>Listing not found.</p>
{% endsingle %}
```

Fetches by `?slug=` (set automatically by URL pattern routing, same as collections).

### Channel URL Patterns

Set a URL pattern in the Configure step (e.g. `/listing/{slug}`). The front router:
1. Queries all active channels with a `url_pattern` set
2. Matches the request URI against each pattern
3. Extracts the slug from the URL
4. Sets `$_GET['slug']` for use by `{% single %}`
5. Renders `{channel-slug}.html` template, falls back to `channel.html`

### Caching and Sync

- Channel data is cached in the `channel_items` SQLite table (all three types)
- Each channel has a configurable TTL (5min, 15min, 1hr, 6hr, daily, manual-only)
- **Auto-sync**: When a template renders a channel loop and the TTL has expired, Outpost syncs automatically before rendering
- **Manual sync**: Click "Sync Now" in Admin → Channels → [channel] → Sync tab
- **Sync log**: Every sync is logged with item counts (added/updated/removed), duration, and errors
- If the external source is down, the last cached data is served

### Pagination Support (REST API only)

REST API channels support two pagination modes for APIs that return paginated data (RSS and CSV channels always return all items in a single fetch):

- **Offset-based**: Uses `page` and `limit` query params. Outpost increments the page until no more data returns.
- **Cursor-based**: Uses a cursor param (e.g. `after`) with the cursor value extracted from the response via a dot-notation path (e.g. `meta.next_cursor`).

### Channel vs Collection Comparison

| Feature | Collections | Channels |
|---|---|---|
| Data source | Admin editor | External REST API, RSS feed, or CSV file |
| Template loop | `{% for item in collection.slug %}` | `{% for item in channel.slug %}` |
| Single item | `{% single var from collection.slug %}` | `{% single var from channel.slug %}` |
| URL patterns | `/post/{slug}` | `/listing/{slug}` |
| Editable in admin | Yes | No (read-only cache) |
| Storage | `collection_items` table | `channel_items` table (cache) |
| Loop options | `limit`, `orderby`, `paginate`, `filteredby`, `related` | `limit`, `orderby` |

### Mixing Channels and Collections

```html
{# Latest blog posts from your CMS #}
{% for post in collection.post limit:3 orderby:published_at %}
  <a href="{{ post.url }}">{{ post.title }}</a>
{% endfor %}

{# Latest property listings from external MLS API #}
{% for listing in channel.mls limit:6 %}
  <div class="listing-card">{{ listing.address }} — {{ listing.price }}</div>
{% endfor %}
```

### Channel Admin API Endpoints

| Action | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `channels` | GET | List all channels with item counts |
| `channels&id=X` | GET | Get single channel with full config |
| `channels` | POST | Create channel (type: api, rss, or csv — immutable) |
| `channels&id=X` | PUT | Update channel |
| `channels&id=X` | DELETE | Delete channel (cascades items + log) |
| `channels/sync&id=X` | POST | Trigger manual sync |
| `channels/sync-log&id=X` | GET | Get sync history (last 50) |
| `channels/discover` | POST | Schema discovery — dispatches by type (API/RSS/CSV) |
| `channels/items&id=X` | GET | Preview cached items (paginated) |

### Channel Database Tables

**`channels`:** `id`, `slug` (UNIQUE), `name`, `type` (default 'api'), `config` (JSON), `field_map` (JSON), `cache_ttl` (seconds), `url_pattern`, `sort_field`, `sort_direction`, `max_items`, `status` ('active'|'paused'), `last_sync_at`, `last_error`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

**`channel_items`:** `id`, `channel_id` (FK → channels.id CASCADE), `external_id`, `slug`, `data` (JSON), `sort_value`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. Indexes: `(channel_id)`, `(channel_id, slug)`, `(channel_id, sort_value)`

**`channel_sync_log`:** `id`, `channel_id` (FK → channels.id CASCADE), `status`, `items_synced`, `items_added`, `items_updated`, `items_removed`, `duration_ms`, `error_message`, `synced_at`

## Sitemap

Auto-generated at `/sitemap.xml`. No configuration needed. Includes all CMS pages and published collection items with `<lastmod>` dates. Base URL auto-detected from HTTP request (or from `OUTPOST_SITE_URL`).

## Analytics

Built-in tracker injected before `</body>` on every page. Fires GET to `/outpost/track.php`. Bot detection via user-agent. No cookies, no fingerprinting, all data in SQLite.

### Analytics Tabs

The Analytics page has 6 tabs:

1. **Traffic** — pageviews, unique visitors, top pages, referrers, device breakdown, geo data (if enabled)
2. **Events** — custom events tracked via `outpost.track(name, properties)`
3. **Goals** — conversion tracking with configurable goals
4. **Search** — internal site search analytics (v2.3)
5. **Content** — content performance cohorts by page publish date (v2.3)
6. **Funnels** — member lifecycle funnel: Visit → Sign Up → Login → Upgrade (v2.3, only visible when members enabled)

### Search Analytics (v2.3)

Track what visitors search for on your site. Two methods:

**JavaScript API** (call from your search results page):
```javascript
// Track a search query and the number of results
outpost.trackSearch('keyword', 5);

// Track when a user clicks a search result
outpost.trackSearchClick('keyword', '/path/clicked');
```

**Automatic detection**: The tracker automatically detects search queries from URL params `?q=`, `?search=`, `?s=` and tracks them (results count defaults to 0 for auto-detected searches).

**API endpoint**: `GET api.php?action=dashboard/search&period=30days`
Returns: `total_searches`, `unique_queries`, `click_rate`, `top_queries`, `zero_result_queries`, `top_clicked`, `chart` (daily trend).

Database table: `analytics_searches` (query, results_count, clicked_path, session_id, created_at).

### Content Performance Cohorts (v2.3)

Groups pages by publish date into 5 cohorts: Last 7 days, Last 30 days, Last 90 days, Last 6 months, Older. Joins with `analytics_hits` to show traffic distribution per cohort.

**API endpoint**: `GET api.php?action=dashboard/cohorts&period=30days`
Returns: Array of cohorts, each with `page_count`, `total_views`, `avg_views_per_page`, `view_share`, `top_pages`.

No new database tables — computed from existing `pages` + `analytics_hits`.

### Goal Funnels (v2.3)

Tracks member lifecycle as a 4-stage funnel:
1. **Visitors** — unique sessions from `analytics_hits`
2. **Sign Ups** — `member_events` where `event_type = 'signup'`
3. **Logins** — distinct users with `event_type = 'login'`
4. **Upgrades** — `event_type = 'role_change'` (role changes to paid tiers)

Events are recorded automatically at registration, login, and role change — no developer action needed.

**API endpoint**: `GET api.php?action=dashboard/funnels&period=30days`
Returns: `stages` array (count + conversion rate per stage), `recent_events` (last 20 member events).

Database table: `member_events` (user_id, event_type, details, session_id, created_at).

### Geo Enrichment (v2.3)

Optional country-level visitor analytics. Double-gated for privacy:
1. Upload a MaxMind GeoLite2-Country `.mmdb` file to `content/data/`
2. Enable via Settings → Advanced → Geo Analytics toggle

When enabled, `track.php` looks up visitor IPs against the mmdb file and stores only the 2-letter country code (e.g., `US`, `DE`, `GB`) in `analytics_hits.country_code`. Raw IPs are never stored.

**Pure-PHP MMDB reader** (`mmdb-reader.php`): ~270 LOC, zero Composer dependencies, supports IPv4 and IPv6.

**API endpoints**:
- `GET api.php?action=dashboard/geo&period=30days` — country breakdown with counts and percentages
- `GET api.php?action=dashboard/geo/status` — check mmdb file status (exists, size, modified date)
- `POST api.php?action=dashboard/geo/upload` — upload mmdb file (multipart form, field name: `mmdb`)
- `DELETE api.php?action=dashboard/geo/delete` — remove mmdb file and disable setting

GA4 integration (optional):
```html
{% if @ga4_id %}
<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id={{ @ga4_id }}"></script>
<script>
  window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
  function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}
  gtag('js', new Date());
  gtag('config', '{{ @ga4_id }}');
</script>
{% endif %}
```

## SEO — `{% seo %}` tag

Place `{% seo %}` once in `head.html`. It outputs a complete SEO block automatically for every page type. No parameters, no configuration, no manual OG tags needed in templates.

```html
<!-- partials/head.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  {% seo %}
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/outpost/themes/my-theme/assets/style.css">
</head>
<body>
```

**What it outputs by page type:**

- **Static page** (`/about`): `<title>`, `<meta name="description">`, `<link rel="canonical">`, `og:type=website`, `og:site_name`, `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:url`, `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image`, `twitter:site`
- **Collection single** (`/post/slug`): All of the above with `og:type=article`, `article:published_time`, `article:author`, plus JSON-LD `Article` schema block
- **Paginated list** (`/blog?page=2`): Adds `<link rel="prev">` and `<link rel="next">`
- **Members/paid page**: Adds `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">`

**Fallback chain:**

| Tag | Priority |
|---|---|
| `<title>` | Page Meta Title → item `title` → page `title` → `@site_name` |
| `meta description` | Page Meta Description → item `excerpt` → first 30 words of `body` → `@site_tagline` |
| `og:image` / `twitter:image` | Page OG Image → item `featured_image` → `@og_image` |
| `og:title` | Same as title, without ` — Site Name` suffix |
| `canonical` | `OUTPOST_SITE_URL` + current path (omitted if constant not set) |
| `og:type` | `article` on single pages, `website` elsewhere |
| `article:author` | Item `author` field → `@author_name` |
| `twitter:site` | `@twitter_handle` global (include `@` prefix) |

**Title format:** `{Page Title} — {Site Name}`. Homepage: Meta Title if set, otherwise just site name.

**Required global fields** (auto-registered on first use, set in Admin → Globals):
- `@og_image` — default Open Graph image (1200×630px recommended)
- `@twitter_handle` — Twitter/X handle with `@` prefix, e.g. `@mysite`
- `@site_tagline` — fallback meta description for pages without one set

**Required config:** `define('OUTPOST_SITE_URL', 'https://example.com');` in `config.php` for canonical URLs.

**JSON-LD Article schema** (collection singles only):
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Article",
  "headline": "Item title",
  "description": "Resolved description",
  "image": "https://example.com/outpost/uploads/featured.jpg",
  "datePublished": "2024-02-01T08:00:00+00:00",
  "dateModified": "2024-02-10T12:00:00+00:00",
  "url": "https://example.com/post/my-article",
  "author": { "@type": "Person", "name": "Author Name" }
}
```

`{% seo %}` replaces `{{ meta.title }}` and `{{ meta.description }}`. Do not use both — `{% seo %}` already outputs `<title>` and description. Individual meta tags still work in legacy themes.

## WordPress Import

Migrate posts from WordPress using the native WXR XML export.

1. In WordPress: Tools → Export → Posts → Download Export File
2. In Outpost: Admin → Import → upload `.xml` file → select target collection → configure options → Import

**What gets imported:** title, body content (sanitized HTML), excerpt, slug, publish date, status, categories/tags (as folder labels if matched).

**Import options:**
- Posts to import: "Published only" or "All" (includes drafts)
- On duplicate: "Skip" (keep existing) or "Overwrite" (replace by slug match)

Images in post content still point to old WordPress domain after import — update manually.

## Code Editor

In-admin theme file editor at Admin → Code. Requires `developer` or higher role.

Features: syntax highlighting (HTML, CSS, JS), tab bar with dirty-state indicator, file tree with create/rename/delete, find-in-files search, Outpost template autocomplete inside `{{ }}` and `{% %}`.

Endpoints added to `code-editor.php`:
- `code/files` GET — list theme code files
- `code/read` GET — read a theme file
- `code/write` POST — write a theme file
- `code/create` POST — create file or folder
- `code/rename` POST — rename/move (validates extension)
- `code/delete` DELETE — recursive for directories
- `code/search` GET `?q=term` — full-text search, max 200 results
- `code/context` GET — returns globals, collections, schema, forms, menus, folders (used by autocomplete and Forge)
- `code/assets` GET `?theme=slug` — list all files in a theme's `assets/` directory (CSS, JS, images, fonts). Returns `{ files: [{ name, path, size }] }`

## Forge (Visual Tag Builder)

Built into the Code Editor. Select HTML in any `.html`/`.htm` file, right-click to open the Forge context menu, or press Cmd+E / Ctrl+E to jump straight to Make Editable. Right-clicking without a selection shows "Insert Asset" and "Insert Component" options.

Forge uses smart detection — it analyzes the selected HTML and reorders the context menu so the most likely action appears first. It also pre-selects the field type in the Make Editable form.

### Detection rules (priority order)

- `<img>`, `<picture>`, `<figure>`, `src="...jpg"` → Make Editable (image)
- `<a href>` → Make Editable (link)
- `<h1>`–`<h6>` → Make Editable (text)
- Short plain text (<100 chars) → Make Editable (text)
- Long plain text (≥100 chars) → Make Editable (textarea)
- HTML with child elements → Make Editable (richtext)
- `<ul>`/`<ol>` with 2+ links → Menu Loop
- `<nav>`, `<header>`, `<footer>` when matching partial exists → **Use Partial: name** (instant include)
- `<nav>` with links → Extract Partial (with menu option)
- `<header>`, `<footer>` → Extract Partial
- `<title>`, `<meta name="description">` → Meta Tag
- `<form>` → Form
- Multiple repeated sibling elements → Collection Loop
- `<section>`, `<article>`, large `<div>` → Conditional

### Seven actions

**Make Editable** — Wraps selection in an output tag. Fields: name (auto-slugified), type (text, richtext, image, link, textarea, select, color, number, date, toggle), scope (page/global), use as default (wrapping default — ON by default for all types including image and link). Output: `{{ field }}`, `{{ field | image }}https://url{{ /field }}`, `{{ @global }}`, or `{{ field }}Default{{ /field }}`.

**Collection Loop** — Wraps selection in `{% for item in collection.slug %}...{% endfor %}`. Fields: collection (dropdown), item variable, limit, order by. **Field mapper**: auto-detects images, headings, paragraphs, and links in selected HTML and maps them to collection fields with auto-guessing.

**Menu Loop** — Wraps navigation links in `{% for link in menu.slug %}...{% endfor %}`. Fields: menu (dropdown of admin menus or free-text slug), link variable. When 2+ links are detected, offers to auto-replace static hrefs and text with `{{ link.url }}` and `{{ link.label }}`.

**Conditional** — Wraps selection in `{% if expression %}...{% endif %}`. Fields: expression, operator (truthy/==/!=), value.

**Extract Partial** — Creates `partials/{name}.html` with the selected content, replaces selection with `{% include 'name' %}`, refreshes file tree, opens the new file in a tab. **Smart nav detection**: when extracting a `<nav>` with 2+ links, offers "Connect to admin menu" checkbox to wrap links in a menu loop automatically. **Apply to all pages**: after extraction, Forge scans other theme files for the same content and offers to replace it in all matching pages at once. **Use Existing Partial**: if a partial with a matching name already exists (e.g. `partials/nav.html` for a `<nav>` selection), "Use Partial: nav" appears at the top of the context menu for instant replacement.

**Meta Tag** — Wraps selection in `{{ meta.title }}...{{ /meta.title }}` or `{{ meta.description }}...{{ /meta.description }}`.

**Form** — Inserts `{% form 'slug' %}`. Dropdown of existing forms from context data.

### Forge Playground

Ships with a 6-page demo website in `themes/forge-playground/` (index, features, blog, gallery, about, contact) — pure HTML/CSS with Unsplash images. No `theme.json` — users "Forge a Theme" via a guided wizard. Includes a reset button to restore all files to pristine state from `.forge-snapshot/` backups.

### Insert Asset

Right-click in an HTML file (even without selecting text) to see "Insert Asset" in the Forge context menu. Opens a searchable modal that auto-discovers files from the theme's `assets/` directory — CSS, JS, images, fonts, and more. Click any file to insert the correct HTML reference tag at the cursor position:

- `.css` → `<link rel="stylesheet" href="/outpost/content/themes/{theme}/assets/{path}">`
- `.js` → `<script src="/outpost/content/themes/{theme}/assets/{path}"></script>`
- images → `<img src="/outpost/content/themes/{theme}/assets/{path}" alt="">`

Powered by the `code/assets` API endpoint which scans the theme's `assets/` directory recursively.

### Forge Theme Wizard

Folders in `themes/` without `theme.json` show a banner in the Code Editor. Click "Forge Theme" to create `theme.json` with name, author, and description via a guided wizard. Also available from the Forge context menu.

### Preview Tabs

Single-clicking a file opens it as a preview tab (italic name). Clicking another file replaces the preview. Double-click or editing pins the tab. Files opened from Find in Files, command palette, or newly created files are always pinned.

### Keyboard shortcut

Cmd+E / Ctrl+E opens Make Editable directly with the field type pre-selected by smart detection. Status bar shows "Cmd+E to tag" hint when a selection exists in an HTML file.

## Settings

Stored as key-value pairs in `settings` table. Edit in Admin → Settings.

Key settings: site name, active theme slug, SMTP host/port/user/pass, from name/email, form notification email, builder sync key.

Read in PHP: `OutpostDB::setting('key', 'default')`

## Configuration (config.php)

All runtime configuration is set via PHP constants in `outpost/config.php`. No `.env`, no YAML.

```php
<?php
// Minimal required config
define('OUTPOST_SITE_URL', 'https://mysite.com');  // no trailing slash
define('OUTPOST_SYNC_KEY', 'your-random-64-char-key');
```

### Full constant reference

| Constant | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OUTPOST_CONTENT_DIR` | `content/` | User data directory containing data, uploads, themes, backups |
| `OUTPOST_DB_PATH` | `content/data/cms.db` | SQLite database path |
| `OUTPOST_SITE_URL` | auto-detected | Canonical URL for sitemap, emails, absolute links |
| `OUTPOST_DEBUG` | `false` | Show PHP errors inline — never true in production |
| `OUTPOST_MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE` | `10485760` (10 MB) | Max upload size in bytes |
| `OUTPOST_WEBP_AUTO_CONVERT` | `true` | Auto-convert JPEG/PNG uploads to WebP |
| `OUTPOST_WEBP_QUALITY` | `85` | WebP compression quality (0–100) |
| `OUTPOST_UPLOADS_DIR` | `content/uploads/` | Media upload directory (symlinked at `outpost/uploads`) |
| `OUTPOST_THEMES_DIR` | `content/themes/` | Themes directory (symlinked at `outpost/themes`) |
| `OUTPOST_BACKUPS_DIR` | `content/backups/` | Backup zip storage directory |
| `OUTPOST_CACHE_DIR` | `cache/` | Cache directory (not under content/ — regenerated) |
| `OUTPOST_CACHE_TTL` | `3600` | HTML page cache TTL in seconds |
| `OUTPOST_TEMPLATE_CACHE` | `true` | Enable compiled template cache (set `false` during dev) |
| `OUTPOST_SESSION_NAME` | `outpost_session` | Admin session cookie name |
| `OUTPOST_MEMBER_SESSION` | `outpost_member` | Member session cookie name |
| `OUTPOST_SESSION_LIFETIME` | `86400` (24h) | Admin session lifetime in seconds |
| `OUTPOST_MEMBER_LIFETIME` | `2592000` (30 days) | Member "remember me" lifetime in seconds |
| `OUTPOST_SYNC_KEY` | (none) | Builder sync API secret key |
| `OUTPOST_SMTP_HOST` | (none) | SMTP hostname |
| `OUTPOST_SMTP_PORT` | `587` | SMTP port (587=STARTTLS, 465=SSL, 25=plain) |
| `OUTPOST_SMTP_USER` | (none) | SMTP username |
| `OUTPOST_SMTP_PASS` | (none) | SMTP password |

## REST API

Base URL: `/outpost/api.php?action=ENDPOINT`

All mutating requests require `X-CSRF-Token` header (token received at login). Session-based auth via cookies.

### Public endpoints (no auth)

| Action | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `auth/forgot` | POST | Send password reset email |
| `auth/reset` | POST | Set new password via token |

### Admin endpoints (require auth)

| Action | Methods | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `auth/login` | POST | Sign in |
| `auth/logout` | POST | Sign out |
| `auth/me` | GET | Current user (includes `update_available` + `latest_version` for admin/super_admin) |
| `pages` | GET, PUT | List and update pages |
| `fields/bulk` | PUT | Bulk page field update |
| `globals` | GET | Global fields |
| `collections` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Collections CRUD |
| `items` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Collection items CRUD (see create example below) |
| `items/inline` | PUT | Partial field update for collection items (on-page editing) |
| `items/bulk-status` | PUT | Bulk update item status (draft/published/pending_review) |
| `items/bulk-delete` | DELETE | Bulk delete items |
| `items/bulk-schedule` | PUT | Bulk schedule items to a specific date/time |
| `items/approve` | PUT | Approve pending_review items (admin+ only) |
| `items/reject` | PUT | Reject pending_review items back to draft (admin+ only) |
| `items/preview-token` | POST | Generate preview token for draft/scheduled items |
| `items/labels-with-counts` | GET | Folders + labels with per-label item counts for a collection |
| `items/bulk-labels` | POST | Bulk add/remove label assignments for items |
| `calendar` | GET | Items by date range for editorial calendar (params: start, end, collection) |
| `folders` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Folders CRUD |
| `labels` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Folder labels CRUD |
| `item-labels` | GET, PUT | Label assignments |
| `media` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Media library (GET: ?folder_id= filter; PUT updates alt_text, focal_x, focal_y, folder_id) |
| `media/upload` | POST | Upload file (optional folder_id form field) |
| `media/transform` | POST | Resize or crop a raster image (GD required) |
| `media/move` | PUT | Move/add/remove media files to/from folders (ids[], folder_id, action: set/add/remove) |
| `media/folders` | GET | Get folder IDs for a media item (media_id param) |
| `media/assign-folders` | PUT | Set all folder assignments for a media item (media_id, folder_ids[]) |
| `media-folders` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Media folder CRUD (max 3 levels deep, auto-generated slugs) |
| `media-folders/bulk` | POST | Create multiple folders at once (names[], parent_id, max 50) |
| `users` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Admin users CRUD |
| `users/grants` | GET, PUT | Collection grants for editor users (GET returns granted IDs, PUT replaces all grants) |
| `users/media-folder-grants` | GET, PUT | Media folder grants for editor users (folder_ids[]) |
| `members` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Member management |
| `menus` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Navigation menus CRUD |
| `loops` | GET, POST, DELETE | Saved query loops |
| `settings` | GET, PUT | Site settings |
| `themes` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Theme management |
| `themes/activate` | PUT | Activate theme |
| `themes/duplicate` | POST | Duplicate theme |
